author: Donna
body:
(I bought it yesterday, and my evaluation of it is below...)

 I have always been afraid of anything "ebook" assuming that it's a scam or marketing thing... or that they are worthless instruments like recipies, pictures, or paperclips used in penny auctions for feedback.

 But after years of trial-and-error of Ebay accounts (and I've learned, lost, and listed an incredible amount over the years) I consider myself having a master's degree in Ebay BS, and bought the book just to see if there are any little tricks I might have overlooked. (When you spend an average cost/waste of $5 everytime you buy a prepaid card, or $35/6mo for a PO Box (or $100 for the year as some PO's have a minimum, which I just paid) then $30something for a comprehensive list of "tricks" is nothing.

 Let me say if you're thinking about buying an Ebay account or starting a second one or are going to make a few to set aside for a "rainy day" then don't even bother until you've read the "Evasion Book" book. It's almost 50 pages, very well done, and not at all the same thing as the ebooks in Ebay penny auctions. There are no sales add-ons like 200 pages of "wholesale sources" at the end, etc.......

 I've seen the ads in my Google searches for the longest of time, but assumed there were so many versions that people bought and sold again so I never bothered. I bought it on a whim 2 days ago (after becoming a member of this site) and HIGHLY recommend it, as there were several things that even I didnt know. 

 It reads like a ".... for Dummies" book in the way that if you dont understand something all you have to do is go back and read it slowly and you will get it. 

 100% NOT clipped and pasted from somewhere else, 100% professional and there are no hidden aggendas for offering it (like other links and downloads inside the book for other products). SOLID info, well organized and I learned a lot from it, even with my years of experience.

 The only down sides that I can think of was that 1) you can't pay for it with Paypal (which I wouldn't anyways, obvious reasons) so you have to have a credit card handy and 2) if it would have said Level 1 ($149) next to the ad I would have bought it a long time ago, at least out of curiosity. Anyone who's either bought an established account or bought a ton of junk for the sake of feedbacks on an account they started themselves knows that a few $100 is not bad, and that Level 1 ($149) is NOTHING when it comes to a secure Ebay account. Sure there are the 5 or 10 things that are common sense, or that you can learn from reading on the internet, but there are a LOT of things that I didnt know. Things I didnt realize DONT matter, and things that DO. 

 Anyway, I wrote all that just so that anyone else on the fence knows that the book is both CHEAP and what I'd consider the BEST. Dont even BOTHER to start or buy another account before breezing through it. 

 I'm sure a lot of other buyers of the book would agree, if just given a place to say so. This website is very informative and great for new thoughts and discussion, but the book is the BEST. DEFINITELY the place to start. 

 Anyone else bought it?

author: jeffweico
body:
I have been on the forums for a few months, and I finally bought the guide. I thought I was pretty knowledgable about creating stealth accounts, but I found several tips that I was unaware of. 

The bottom line is, you could pick up much of this info by reading the forums, but it would take a long time, and you would have to ask several questions. Even then, this guide could probably teach you a thing or two.

As a business decision, buying this was a no-brainer. It takes time and money to create a stealth account PROPERLY and then you have to know how to work it to lessen the chances of getting shut down. This guide will easily save me much more than its cost in the future. It can also be very enlightening in other ways, it could give you ideas. 

You could even buy a ready made account, but then what? One slight mistake, and it becomes instantly worthless. Whether you buy an account or create your own, this guide will help. At Level 1 ($149), it is cheap for the knowledge gained. 

With this base of klnowledge, you can then use the forums more effectively, in order to keep up to date on the latest developments, rather than spending tons of time trying to piece things together. 

Think of it this way... Is it worth Level 1 ($149) to you to keep an account active? Or, is it worth Level 1 ($149) to know how to lessen your risk of being banned by PayPal with them holding your funds for 180 days? 

I personally feel this guide provides 30% of its value by teaching you how to create a stealth account and 70% in teaching you how to keep it. 

I wish I had bought it sooner. Reading the forums is great, but it is not really a substitute for the guide. The forums merely enhance its value. 

Just my opinion....

Quote: 
					Originally Posted by waterproof 

To be successful with opening a Stealth account the person must READ...READ...the Book and then very patiently do one thing at a time. Log everything or write everything you do down on paper or whatever your method may be..

waterproof...... The most important part of this is to write down EVERYTHING! It is probably also the most overlooked. 

If you write everything down and keep a log, you will have something to refer to that helps you to not make mistakes. 

And if something goes wronmg, you can refer back to it to see what exactly went wrong. 

I neglected this on the first account I set up in my mad rush to get it done. Then, I had a potential problem and I could not remember exactly what I did. ACCOUNT CLOSED! 

Now, I keep a notebook for each account logging EVERYTHING. This has saved me many headaches. You think you will remember something, but a month goes by and - I promise you - you will have forgotten something. 

Read the Evasion Book

Keep Records of Everything!

Ask questions on the forum if you need help. 

That is the best way to get the most from the guide. 

Follow those steps and the rest is easy. Ignore them, and kiss your account(s) goodbye!

author: vitamingal
body:
Quote: 
					Originally Posted by Donna (I bought it yesterday, and my evaluation of it is below...)

 I have always been afraid of anything "ebook" assuming that it's a scam or marketing thing... or that they are worthless instruments like recipies, pictures, or paperclips used in penny auctions for feedback.

 But after years of trial-and-error of Ebay accounts (and I've learned, lost, and listed an incredible amount over the years) I consider myself having a master's degree in Ebay BS, and bought the book just to see if there are any little tricks I might have overlooked. (When you spend an average cost/waste of $5 everytime you buy a prepaid card, or $35/6mo for a PO Box (or $100 for the year as some PO's have a minimum, which I just paid) then $30something for a comprehensive list of "tricks" is nothing.

 Let me say if you're thinking about buying an Ebay account or starting a second one or are going to make a few to set aside for a "rainy day" then don't even bother until you've read the "Evasion Book" book. It's almost 50 pages, very well done, and not at all the same thing as the ebooks in Ebay penny auctions. There are no sales add-ons like 200 pages of "wholesale sources" at the end, etc.......

 I've seen the ads in my Google searches for the longest of time, but assumed there were so many versions that people bought and sold again so I never bothered. I bought it on a whim 2 days ago (after becoming a member of this site) and HIGHLY recommend it, as there were several things that even I didnt know. 

 It reads like a ".... for Dummies" book in the way that if you dont understand something all you have to do is go back and read it slowly and you will get it. 

 100% NOT clipped and pasted from somewhere else, 100% professional and there are no hidden aggendas for offering it (like other links and downloads inside the book for other products). SOLID info, well organized and I learned a lot from it, even with my years of experience.

 The only down sides that I can think of was that 1) you can't pay for it with Paypal (which I wouldn't anyways, obvious reasons) so you have to have a credit card handy and 2) if it would have said Level 1 ($149) next to the ad I would have bought it a long time ago, at least out of curiosity. Anyone who's either bought an established account or bought a ton of junk for the sake of feedbacks on an account they started themselves knows that a few $100 is not bad, and that Level 1 ($149) is NOTHING when it comes to a secure Ebay account. Sure there are the 5 or 10 things that are common sense, or that you can learn from reading on the internet, but there are a LOT of things that I didnt know. Things I didnt realize DONT matter, and things that DO. 

 Anyway, I wrote all that just so that anyone else on the fence knows that the book is both CHEAP and what I'd consider the BEST. Dont even BOTHER to start or buy another account before breezing through it. 

 I'm sure a lot of other buyers of the book would agree, if just given a place to say so. This website is very informative and great for new thoughts and discussion, but the book is the BEST. DEFINITELY the place to start. 

 Anyone else bought it? I bought it and for sure it helped me. I got thrown off of paypal after 10 yrs so i had no memory of how to get back on...I bought 5 combo's from Doughboykilla, and he came thru like a charm....I still have questions for him ( he probably hates me) but even the book overlooks a few things, like how to ship small items to yourself to help alittle bit with the feedback, becaues the names they provide you are ⊗⊗⊗⊗ with ⊗⊗⊗⊗ address...lol.....But i am on my 2nd time reading the book and reccommend everyone get it without a doubt

author: biggunn
body:
It can be a bit hard to access the router info... The best advice you can get is to simply Google search things like this. "How do I access my router page for my linksys (or belkin or whatever brand) router?" Almost any question you can think of has already been asked and answered. 

I hope you already found your answer. If not to access your router page: Your computer must be directly connected to the router, no wireless. Pull up IE or firefox. Type in the address 192.168.1.1 which is the routers IP and press enter. A username and pw box should pop up. If you've never accessed this screen the username should be 'admin' and the password 'password' 

If you need more info on using the router settings there is tons of info and screenshots all over Google. 

Good luck!

body:
Eb changes things all the time so you have to keep up to date with all the info on the forums. The biggest recent change I've noticed is that it is no longer recommended and almost frowned upon to clear your cookies. Eb apparently likes to read the same cookies for your accounts each time you log in. The new stealth method is to have separate windows user accounts on your computer for each stealth account.

Good luck!

author: livelifewild
body:
I've been doing the ebay/paypal run around game for years. Mainly because when I was younger (18 yrs old) I didnt know much about anything and made two accounts and bid on each other thinking it would be cool to make more money! lol stupid

anyway after that needless to say it got shut down, and of course knew nothing about ips or anything. and got my moms info to open an account then my dads....lol so on. Never changing computers or browsers/users ips nothing. So over time my family members have been gone thru. Til I got a cheap ebook on ebaying again. It helped I got back on, plus with some LEARNING over the years I managed to keep that account.

Over time it got suspended, they usually always do its just a matter of how long you get to keep them. (and I say that they usually do, because even completely 100% legit all real information accounts get closed for the stupidest reasons, sometimes for no reason other than ebay decided to pms that day).

Well the two main ones went down, and one of the little ones. And needless to say that panic feeling kicks in and BAM I needed to find more help! lol Modees website/forum doesnt seem to be functioning anymore so I had to search for another source. Found @refundgod and his Evasion Book. And loved it!

 Alot of stuff I already knew, but there was a lot of helpful detailed tidbits that I didnt even think of. I recommend anyone and everyone to get it no matter how well educated you are in ebay suspension and accounts. There is going to be something in it that you dont know.

There were only two things I wish were in the ebook (I dont know if it wasnt included because it isnt updated to include this info just yet)

1) But for me a HUGE help would be a better way (or anyway) to keep from getting the dreadful 48 hr email to call for identity verification. Which really if its a fictitious name, isnt hard to verify. But its a pain in the butt, and doesnt guarantee it wont be suspended/closed down a few days later even if they say alls well and lift the restriction.

2) Getting around, or past, the new account 10 item limit. (sooner than just waiting several months and crossing your fingers that when you get close to your limit they will raise it lol)

So anyway point: BUY THE BOOK! 

and if anyone has some info/help on those 2 points I WOULD SOOOOO Appreciate it!

author: waterproof
body:
I purchased Stealth 800'ish days ago and I am still running strong! I have said before that Stealth has been nothing short of a blessing for me! Besides being on the site, I break no rules, and still make a very comfortable second income!

At the time of my departure from Ebay, like most of us for NO reason, I was in dire need to sell my products/items. Although they are 'higher end' items...I make a dollar and so does both Ebay/PP. So we all profit and everyone is happy until you make the slightest mistake and you get MC'd!

To anyone NEW reading this....please READ your STEALTH over a few times before asking questions. There are plenty of Professionals here to help and these guys really know their stuff, but please read first then ask.

Secondly, take your time! I made it a point to read, ask questions, read more, WRITE DOWN & TAKE NOTES, be organized, and don't get in such a hurry to sell. I'm telling you from experience, that if you get GREEDY without having and recalling the knowledge of Stealth, you'll go down.

I read, I asked my fair share of questions and GreenBean and many others can tell you, but I waited 6 months before I was sure and ready to go and I'm still selling and buying. 

Good Luck

waterproof

author: johnnyleeds
body:
Hi Folks!

I can only add to what people have already said, I have a very old version of the Evasion Book manual and it opened my eyes to issues I wasnt even aware of. Without the manual you might eventually get an ebay / paypal stealth account set up, but probably along the way you would make some fundamental errors that could mean your new accounts get linked to your old accounts and thus suspended. 

The Evasion Book manual gives you a comprehensive step-by-step guide to setting up not just your ebay / paypal seller accounts, but ensuring your PC is correctly configured so that you dont get your accounts linked / suspended again. Its a tour de force achievement by this @refundgod guy and combined with this wonderful forum he has saved our collective necks from poverty and the dole queue - I kid you not! 

If you are considering buying it because you've just lost your ebay / paypal account and you desperately need to start selling again ASAP then DO NOT HESITATE - BUY IT, READ IT, DO IT NOW! I dont know how much the manual costs right now but I promise you it would still be cheap at ten times the price. Just look at all the glowing reviews here, ask some of the people in the forum, I doubt you will hear from one person who doesnt thank God that this guide exists. 

Thanks @refundgod for helping me to be able to work as a self-employed Ebay Antiques & Collectables trader rather than been stuck in some 9-5 minimum wage hellhole call-centre / factory. I wouldnt have been able to do it without yourself and the people on your forum. 

Kindest regards

Johnny

author: pickanitem
body:
is this an ebook or an actuall book

 

 Quote: 
					Originally Posted by Donna (I bought it yesterday, and my evaluation of it is below...)

 I have always been afraid of anything "ebook" assuming that it's a scam or marketing thing... or that they are worthless instruments like recipies, pictures, or paperclips used in penny auctions for feedback.

 But after years of trial-and-error of Ebay accounts (and I've learned, lost, and listed an incredible amount over the years) I consider myself having a master's degree in Ebay BS, and bought the book just to see if there are any little tricks I might have overlooked. (When you spend an average cost/waste of $5 everytime you buy a prepaid card, or $35/6mo for a PO Box (or $100 for the year as some PO's have a minimum, which I just paid) then $30something for a comprehensive list of "tricks" is nothing.

 Let me say if you're thinking about buying an Ebay account or starting a second one or are going to make a few to set aside for a "rainy day" then don't even bother until you've read the "Evasion Book" book. It's almost 50 pages, very well done, and not at all the same thing as the ebooks in Ebay penny auctions. There are no sales add-ons like 200 pages of "wholesale sources" at the end, etc.......

 I've seen the ads in my Google searches for the longest of time, but assumed there were so many versions that people bought and sold again so I never bothered. I bought it on a whim 2 days ago (after becoming a member of this site) and HIGHLY recommend it, as there were several things that even I didnt know. 

 It reads like a ".... for Dummies" book in the way that if you dont understand something all you have to do is go back and read it slowly and you will get it. 

 100% NOT clipped and pasted from somewhere else, 100% professional and there are no hidden aggendas for offering it (like other links and downloads inside the book for other products). SOLID info, well organized and I learned a lot from it, even with my years of experience.

 The only down sides that I can think of was that 1) you can't pay for it with Paypal (which I wouldn't anyways, obvious reasons) so you have to have a credit card handy and 2) if it would have said Level 1 ($149) next to the ad I would have bought it a long time ago, at least out of curiosity. Anyone who's either bought an established account or bought a ton of junk for the sake of feedbacks on an account they started themselves knows that a few $100 is not bad, and that Level 1 ($149) is NOTHING when it comes to a secure Ebay account. Sure there are the 5 or 10 things that are common sense, or that you can learn from reading on the internet, but there are a LOT of things that I didnt know. Things I didnt realize DONT matter, and things that DO. 

 Anyway, I wrote all that just so that anyone else on the fence knows that the book is both CHEAP and what I'd consider the BEST. Dont even BOTHER to start or buy another account before breezing through it. 

 I'm sure a lot of other buyers of the book would agree, if just given a place to say so. This website is very informative and great for new thoughts and discussion, but the book is the BEST. DEFINITELY the place to start. 

 Anyone else bought it?

body:
I haven't used the book for a while, but I just tried to open it, and it's not working.

This is what happens:

"The publisher could not be verified..." then I click on Run

Something appears for a second which says Open Link and OK, then I see the blue loading bar, and then it closes immediately, the book won't even load, at least I don't see it.

Edit: I made a screenshot using the print screen function, and finally I was able to see what it says: "Your Secure-Ebook product needs to upgraded to a more recent version. To start the upgrade process, click on the "Open Link" button below, and follow the instructions". It closes so fast that I don't even have a chance to click on it.

When I check the processes in Task Manager, I see that two AcroRd32.exe and two eBay-Stealth.exe are running but as I said earlier the book/pdf document closes immediately.

Does anyone else have the same problem? I'm able to open Evasion Book, but not Evasion Book.

Edit: finally I was able to click on the open link tab, and it was upgraded successfully, so now everything works perfectly and everybody is happy

author: arwoodco
body:
Quote: 
					Originally Posted by lyl797 Now I just have to deal with all those eBay scums that buying for fraud .. shoooot .. these guys are getting ridiculous day after day .. Here is something to consider.. Looking at ebay as a place to buy things, rather than sell. 

Same also with Amazon. As the leverage is on the side of the buyer. 

So, what I was doing for a couple years was selling on my own website and then buying on ebay and amazon and having it shipped to customers. 

Playing price comparison games. Worked pretty well. 

As long as you can avoid the silly 10% for 90 days rolling reserve that paypal seems to inflict on bigger sellers. 

At any rate, the point is.. Then, if you ever have a dispute, you can just goto Ebay or Amazon where you purchased and make the vendor take a return. 

Also, the good news is you have less disputes when you sell directly with paypal. 

And also, the power is in your favor, as Paypal does not cover buyer purchases outside of ebay the same as on ebay. 

Outside of Ebay, if someone makes a not as described dispute, you often win. 

Outside of Ebay: 

1. Not as described dispute = you often win. 

2. Non-receipt = just provide tracking. 

3. Not as described chargeback = well, you still loose here most of the time. 

4. Non-receipt chargeback = just provide tracking. 

On Ebay: 

1. Customer complains for any reason whatsoever. 

2. You are screwed.

body:
I keep seeing this question come up. YES, it really is worth it. The guide works well. And, yes, you could learn stealth for free by browsing around the forums. But it will take you a lot longer. 

How many sales could you make on an Amazon account in a week? How many weeks would it take you to put the pieces together to figure it out on your own? Is it worth it to create an account only to lose it because you missed something (or, more likely, several things)? 

The guide does not cost very much for the value it provides. If you have items with a decent profit margin, you could even cover the cost of the guide in a sale or two.

Please note that I do not receive a single penny if you buy the guide. It does not benefit me at all. But it might avoid a lot of frustration for the buyer!

I would add that I understand the skepticism. Most e-books out there are total crap. But @refundgod's guides are not like that at all. 

The guide presents a practical, step-by-step method to get back to selling on Amazon. I still refer to it sometimes, even though I have created more than 30 stealth Amazon accounts.

body:

After searching, to apply multiple EIN seems the solution, but anyone know AMZ link account by the same tax ID? It seems not easy to get multiple EIN

Thanks! I usually make new LLCs for each account, with new EIN number for each of them. You still have to put who owns the LLC when filing out the amazon paperwork, but not the owner's SSN. Just a name. There are many John Smith's in the USA that own many LLCs... no linking there. (John Smith is just an example, doesn't matter what your name is or how unique. Only SSN or EIN, addressed, and etc.)

author: roboulos
body:
Quote: 
					Originally Posted by Beautiful Which step are you on? I'm not who you replied to, but I have been trying to figure this out for myself and would appreciate your input.

My goal is to create a stealth Amazon Sellers account that ultimately I have full control over in terms of documents and identification.

Here are the main steps to what I am thinking of trying:
 Form a Wyoming LLC in my name (WY offers privacy) Obtain EIN for LLC Obtain business mail forwarding address in WY Obtain WY Phone Number Create new Gmail, as outlined in Evasion Book Use Payoneer to receive funds 

I would create the account as a business with all of the information being legitimate. I would provide the EIN for the tax interview, everything matching the LLC. However, for the requested documents, I would use a service such as secondeye to provide a utility bill that matches the WY address, and a bank statement that matches the Payoneer number. For the Photo ID, I would use the same service to send in a passport with my name (not sure if I should use my picture) because my existing passport has already been seen by Amazon.

I have also considered using a DBA, but as a sole proprietor, I can only get one EIN, and it does not seem to afford me the same privacy that an LLC does. 

Is there anything about this that stands out as incorrect? For example, should I not use Payoneer, or should I not risk sending in illegitimate photo ID if I'm going through all this trouble to establish an LLC in WY? Maybe I should I take advantage of the privacy benefits and use a friends name/legitimate passport as the 'face' and 'name', assuming Amazon can't see who the real owner is for a WY LLC? The downside that I see to that is it is not in my true name, but ultimately as the true owner of the LLC I still do have control.
